recipes.title: Masgouf
recipes.description: Iraq's national dish of butterflied carp seasoned with tamarind and turmeric, slow-roasted upright over open flame.
steps: Butterfly the carp from the back, leaving belly skin attached, and remove the spine.
Whisk olive oil, tamarind paste, turmeric, cumin, salt, and pepper into a paste.
Rub the marinade thoroughly across the flesh and let stand 30 minutes.
Build a hot wood fire and lean the fish skin-side out toward the flames using a metal frame.
Roast slowly for 45 to 60 minutes until the flesh is opaque and lightly smoked.
Move the fish skin-side down onto embers for the last 5 minutes to crisp the skin.
Serve on a platter with sliced tomatoes, onions, and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Tashreeb
recipes.description: Rustic Iraqi dish of torn flatbread soaked in spiced lamb broth with chickpeas and tender chunks of meat.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown cubed lamb in oil with chopped onions until well caramelized.
Stir in tomato paste, baharat, and cumin and cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, soaked chickpeas, dried limes, salt, and 2 liters of water.
Simmer covered for 2 hours until lamb is fork-tender and chickpeas are creamy.
Tear flatbread into rough pieces and pile in the bottom of a deep serving bowl.
Ladle the hot broth over the bread to soak, then top with chunks of lamb and chickpeas.

recipes.title: Iraqi Dolma
recipes.description: Iraqi mixed stuffed vegetables, grape leaves, peppers, and onions filled with spiced rice and meat, layered and slow-simmered.
steps: Rinse rice and soak 30 minutes; mix with minced lamb, tomato paste, baharat, salt, and oil.
Hollow tomatoes, peppers, and courgettes carefully without breaking the walls.
Peel onions and boil 5 minutes; separate into individual layers.
Stuff every vegetable and onion layer with the rice mixture, leaving room for expansion.
Roll grape leaves around the same filling into tight cigars.
Pack everything snugly into a heavy pot, brushed with oil and lined with sliced potato or extra leaves.
Mix lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, remaining oil, salt, and 750 ml water and pour over.
Weight with a plate and simmer covered for 1.5 hours; rest 15 minutes before inverting onto a tray.

recipes.title: Kubba
recipes.description: Iraqi torpedo-shaped dumplings of bulgur and minced meat, fried golden with a juicy spiced lamb filling.
steps: Soak bulgur in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain and squeeze dry.
Process bulgur with the minced beef, salt, and half the baharat into a smooth paste.
Sauté finely chopped onions in 2 tablespoons oil until soft, then add minced lamb and brown.
Stir in pine nuts, cinnamon, and remaining baharat; cool the filling completely.
Wet hands and shape balls of the bulgur shell, hollow with a finger, fill with the lamb mixture, and seal into torpedoes.
Heat oil to 180C and fry kubba in batches for 5 minutes until deep golden.
Drain on paper and serve hot with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Quzi
recipes.description: Iraqi feast of slow-roasted whole lamb served on a bed of spiced rice with nuts, raisins, and fried onions.
steps: Rub the lamb shoulder with baharat, salt, and oil and let marinate 1 hour.
Sit the lamb on sliced onions in a deep tray with 500 ml water; cover and roast at 150C for 4 hours.
Uncover and roast another 30 minutes to brown the surface.
Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes, then boil 6 minutes in salted water and drain.
Layer the par-cooked rice in a pot with butter and turmeric and steam covered for 30 minutes.
Toast almonds and pine nuts in butter until golden, then add raisins to plump.
Pile the rice on a large platter, sit the roasted lamb on top, and scatter the nuts and raisins.

recipes.title: Margat Bamia
recipes.description: Iraqi okra and lamb stew in a tangy tomato broth brightened with dried lime and lemon.
steps: Brown cubed lamb with chopped onions in oil until caramelized.
Stir in tomato paste, baharat, and minced garlic; cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, dried limes pierced with a knife, salt, and 1 liter of water.
Simmer for 1.5 hours until the lamb is tender.
Trim okra stems carefully and add to the stew with lemon juice.
Simmer another 20 minutes until okra is tender but still bright green.
Adjust seasoning and serve with white rice.

recipes.title: Pacha
recipes.description: Traditional Iraqi dish of slow-cooked lamb's head, trotters, and stuffed tripe in a clear, garlicky broth.
steps: Scrub trotters and tripe thoroughly and blanch in boiling water for 10 minutes; drain and rinse.
Soak rice 30 minutes and mix with chopped onion, baharat, turmeric, and salt.
Stuff the cleaned tripe with the rice mixture and stitch closed with kitchen twine.
Place trotters, tongues, and stuffed tripe in a large pot; cover with water and bring to a slow simmer.
Skim the foam, add halved garlic heads, salt, and a pinch of turmeric.
Simmer very gently for 5 to 6 hours until everything is fall-apart tender.
Slice the tongue and stuffed tripe and arrange on a platter with the trotters and broth.
Serve with lemon wedges, extra garlic, and flatbread.

recipes.title: Kleicha
recipes.description: Iraqi national cookies of cardamom-scented dough rolled around date paste, walnuts, or coconut filling.
steps: Warm the milk with sugar and yeast and let foam 10 minutes.
Rub butter into flour, cardamom, fennel, nigella, and salt until sandy.
Add yeast mixture and knead a smooth dough; rest 1 hour covered.
Soften date paste with a little warm water and a pinch of cardamom; mix with chopped walnuts.
Roll the dough into a long thin rectangle, spread the date filling, and roll into a log.
Slice the log into 1 cm pinwheels and place on a lined tray.
Brush with beaten egg and bake at 180C for 18 minutes until lightly golden.
Cool on a rack before serving with tea.

recipes.title: Margat Fasolia
recipes.description: Iraqi white bean and lamb stew slow-simmered in tomato broth with garlic and dried lime.
steps: Soak white beans overnight and drain.
Brown cubed lamb with chopped onion in oil.
Stir in tomato paste, baharat, and minced garlic and cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, soaked beans, dried lime, salt, and 1.2 liters water.
Simmer covered for 1.5 hours until the beans are creamy and lamb is tender.
Adjust seasoning and reduce uncovered for 10 minutes if too thin.
Serve over white rice with chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Timman Bagilla
recipes.description: Iraqi rice pilaf of tender fava beans and dill folded into buttery basmati rice.
steps: Rinse rice and soak in salted water for 30 minutes.
Blanch shelled fava beans for 2 minutes, then peel off the outer skin.
Boil rice in plenty of salted water for 6 minutes until al dente; drain.
Heat oil and butter in a non-stick pot, then layer rice with fava beans and chopped dill.
Wrap the lid in a tea towel and steam on low heat for 45 minutes.
Fluff gently and serve with grilled meat or fish.

recipes.title: Samoon
recipes.description: Iraqi diamond-shaped white bread with a crisp crust and soft, airy crumb, baked daily in stone ovens.
steps: Mix yeast and sugar into warm water and let foam 10 minutes.
Combine flour and salt, then add the yeast water and olive oil; knead 10 minutes until smooth.
Cover and rise 1.5 hours until doubled.
Divide into 8 pieces and shape each into a diamond by stretching and pinching the ends.
Place on a floured tray, cover, and prove 30 minutes.
Sprinkle with nigella seeds and slash a line down the center.
Bake on a preheated stone at 240C for 10 minutes until deep golden with a thin crust.
Cool on a rack so the crust crisps.

recipes.title: Maglooba
recipes.description: Iraqi upside-down rice with spiced chicken, fried eggplant, and potato, dramatically inverted onto a serving platter.
steps: Salt sliced eggplant and rest 30 minutes, then pat dry.
Fry eggplant slices and potato slices in oil until golden, then drain.
Brown chicken with chopped onion, baharat, turmeric, and salt.
Add 1 liter of water and simmer chicken 30 minutes; reserve broth and chicken separately.
Layer eggplant, potato, sliced tomato, and chicken on the bottom of a heavy pot.
Top with rinsed and drained rice; pour over enough hot chicken broth to cover by 2 cm.
Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on lowest heat for 35 minutes until rice is tender.
Rest 10 minutes, then invert the pot onto a large platter to reveal the layered dome.
